skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: GPT-Image-2, FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image, gpt image, openai image, chatgpt image
skill.summary: Generate AI images with 50+ models including GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and more via the inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. The 43 'external_commands' flags are markdown code fences misinterpreted as Ruby backticks. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flag on line 3 is YAML frontmatter, not code. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate service endpoints. No command injection vectors, no data exfiltration, no malicious patterns detected.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3. This is YAML frontmatter describing the skill, not code. No cryptographic algorithms are present in this skill documentation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is clearly YAML frontmatter containing only descriptive text. No cryptographic code exists in the file.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 43 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across SKILL.md. These are markdown code fences (```bash) misinterpreted as Ruby backticks. The skill contains only documentation with CLI examples, no actual code execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are markdown code blocks showing belt CLI commands. No actual shell execution, Ruby backticks, or command injection vulnerabilities exist.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 10 'Hardcoded URL' instances. These are legitimate service endpoints (inference.sh), documentation links, and example URLs expected for a CLI skill. No security concern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to official inference.sh service endpoints and documentation. Expected and appropriate for a CLI skill reference.

content.value_statement: Creating custom images for projects requires expensive subscriptions or complex local setup. This skill connects Claude, Codex, and Claude Code directly to 50+ AI image models through the inference.sh CLI, enabling instant image generation without leaving your development environment.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 50+ AI models including GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream
Edit existing images with inpainting, background changes, and style transfers
Upscale images to professional quality using Topaz upscaler
Render accurate text in images using models like Seedream 3.0 and Reve
Apply custom LoRA styles for consistent brand aesthetics
Generate product photos with studio lighting and professional quality
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and CLI installation (belt)
API usage costs apply based on model chosen (varies by provider)
Output quality depends on prompt engineering; complex scenes may need multiple attempts
Some models have rate limits or may be temporarily unavailable

use_cases.title: Product mockups and e-commerce visuals
use_cases.description: Generate professional product photography without expensive photo shoots. Create mockups with consistent lighting and backgrounds for catalogs and marketing materials.

use_cases.title: Concept art and creative exploration
use_cases.description: Rapidly iterate on visual concepts during early design phases. Explore multiple art styles and compositions before committing to a direction.

use_cases.title: Documentation and tutorial illustrations
use_cases.description: Generate custom diagrams, flowcharts, and illustrations for technical documentation without stock photo limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-image
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple image generation for basic concepts

prompt_templates.title: Product photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of {product_name}, {material}, with {lighting_type} lighting, on a {background_type} background

prompt_templates.title: Style transfer
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this image with {art_style} aesthetic, maintaining the {subject_type} while applying {mood} mood
prompt_templates.scenario: Image editing with specific art direction

prompt_templates.title: Scene composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a {scene_type} featuring {main_subject} with {secondary_elements}, {lighting_description}, {camera_angle} view, {resolution} resolution
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex scene generation with detailed requirements

output_examples.input: Generate a professional product photo of wireless headphones on a white marble surface with soft studio lighting
output_examples.output: A high-resolution product image showing sleek wireless headphones centered on white marble, dramatic soft lighting casting subtle shadows, clean and minimal aesthetic suitable for premium e-commerce listing

output_examples.input: Create a cyberpunk cityscape at night with neon signs in Japanese, flying vehicles, and rain-slicked streets
output_examples.output: A detailed cyberpunk cityscape featuring towering buildings with holographic neon advertisements in Japanese script, flying cars navigating the rain-soaked streets below, moody atmospheric lighting with vibrant color accents

output_examples.input: Design a poster that says HELLO WORLD in bold retro typography on a textured paper background
output_examples.output: A poster design with the text 'HELLO WORLD' rendered in bold, retro-style typography with slight texture and wear, placed on a cream-colored paper background with subtle fiber texture visible

best_practices: Start with specific, descriptive prompts including subject, setting, style, and lighting details
Use the right model for the task: Seedream for text rendering, FLUX for artistic styles, GPT-Image-2 for editing
Generate multiple variations and iterate; save successful prompts as templates for consistent results
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make it look good' without specifying style, mood, or composition
Do not rely on single generation attempts for critical assets; always generate multiple options
Avoid overloading prompts with contradictory requirements; prioritize clarity over comprehensiveness

faq.question: What models are available through this skill?
faq.answer: Over 50 models are available including GPT-Image-2, FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, and many more. Run `belt app list --category image` to see all options.

faq.question: Do I need an API key or account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an inference.sh account. Run `belt login` to authenticate, then manage your API credits on the inference.sh dashboard.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text in images?
faq.answer: Use Seedream 3.0 or Reve for accurate text rendering. These models are specifically optimized for generating readable text in images.

faq.question: How do I edit an existing image?
faq.answer: Pass the image URL using the images parameter: `belt app run openai/gpt-image-2 --input '{"prompt": "change background", "images": ["https://your-image.jpg"]}'`

faq.question: What are the costs for different models?
faq.answer: Costs vary by model. FLUX Klein 4B is ultra-cheap at $0.0001 per image, while premium models like GPT-Image-2 cost more. Check inference.sh pricing for current rates.

faq.question: How do I upscale images?
faq.answer: Use the Topaz upscaler: `belt app run falai/topaz-image-upscaler --input '{"image_url": "https://your-image.jpg"}'` for professional-quality upscaling.
